programmes.show_description: Another Life: When a man's body is found in Denton Canal, Frost unravels the mystery of a missing dance partner and a web of intrigue involving blackmail, envy and murder.
Lost Property: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy bus driver and his conductor pal. Stan and Jack find themselves involved in a fishy tale about a lost diamond.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
There Goes the Bride: Classic British sitcom. When Aunt Eleanor announces her engagement after a whirlwind romance, Simon faces the prospect of having a brand-new uncle.
Stan's Uniform: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy driver and his conductor pal at the Luxton Bus Company. Stan is finally issued with a new uniform.
Pollard admits his money troubles to Val when she cancels the order he was relying on for cash. Gennie tries to live up to her name, and Donna is happy with Marlon's success.
Val tells a shocked Diane she is considering selling her share of the pub to help Eric. Gennie tries to sell Viv a smoothie maker in an attempt to prove her worth as a Dingle.
Tina pays David a surprise visit, raising his hopes of a reconciliation before his court appearance. Roy and Becky wait for trouble to brew as the builders arrive with their vans.
Celebrations for Gail's 50th birthday fall by the wayside as David faces the magistrates for sentencing. Ryan tells his grandparents about Alex, and Tony manages to appease Roy.
Endangered Species: Frost investigates when a couple find the body of a stranger in their bed and discovers a cache of rare and exotic animals while tackling a murder case.
Primal Instinct: Drama series. When an ex-policeman is murdered, Nick is seconded to a Scotland Yard team who put local noses out of joint and force Nick to put his career at risk.
Keep on Running: Kate insists that her feelings be considered when Nick is offered a promotion, and the couple's problems are not helped when Nick is arrested on a visit to London.
Things That Go Bump in the Night: The murder of an undertaker takes Barnaby and Scott to a village where residents are divided about the rights and wrongs of a spiritualist church.
Eric is forced to break the news to the workers that the factory will have to close - setting off a chain of events that see it go up in smoke.
Gail returns home to drown her sorrows as the reality of what's happened sinks in. David tries to contact Tina after he's led from the courtroom and Jason has bad news for Becky.
David struggles to adjust to life behind bars, but finds solace when a worried Gail pays him a visit. Roy keeps a tight rein on Tony, and Becky shows her gratitude to Jason.
Secrets: Kate is confronted by a young pregnant village girl while working with the elderly local GP, and Nick takes a call saying that a flasher has been sighted on the moors.
End of the Line: Greengrass tries to take advantage of the situation when Nick investigates a break-in at a remote farm, and a visitor from London rekindles unpleasant memories.
Dirty: The murder of a cleaner whose death occurred several hours after the killer blow landed puts DCI Vera Stanhope on an intriguing trail while piecing his final hours together.
Harvest: Crime drama. The discovery of a 2000-year-old body offers a new lead in a missing persons case.
Endangered Species: Frost investigates when a couple find the body of a stranger in their bed and discovers a cache of rare and exotic animals while tackling a murder case.
Stan's Uniform: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy driver and his conductor pal at the Luxton Bus Company. Stan is finally issued with a new uniform.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Two Fools and Their Money: Classic British sitcom. The antiques business suffers when a recession hits, and Simon and Oliver both feel the pinch when a new bank manager takes over.
The Strain: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy bus driver and his conductor pal. Stan decides to do some weight-training... and uses his girlfriend Doreen as the weight.
Eric is forced to break the news to the workers that the factory will have to close - setting off a chain of events that see it go up in smoke.
Gail returns home to drown her sorrows as the reality of what's happened sinks in. David tries to contact Tina after he's led from the courtroom and Jason has bad news for Becky.
David struggles to adjust to life behind bars, but finds solace when a worried Gail pays him a visit. Roy keeps a tight rein on Tony, and Becky shows her gratitude to Jason.
Care and Protection: The very first episode of the police drama series featuring DI Jack Frost. The search for a missing child unearths a crime that took place 30 years earlier.
Secrets: Kate is confronted by a young pregnant village girl while working with the elderly local GP, and Nick takes a call saying that a flasher has been sighted on the moors.
End of the Line: Greengrass tries to take advantage of the situation when Nick investigates a break-in at a remote farm, and a visitor from London rekindles unpleasant memories.
Dead in the Water: Barnaby and Scott dig into the luxury lifestyles of the rowing set when the annual Midsomer Regatta is brought to a standstill after a body is found in a river.
Carl worries about Donald when he has a funny turn and refuses to go to the hospital. Val accuses Pearl of causing the fire, and the villagers learn about Pollard's plans.
Terry confronts Matthew about the apartment development - warning the businessman he will contest the plans. Marlon is offered a new job, and Carl tells Donald to take it easy.
David puts on a brave face for Gail's sake, but breaks down in tears when she leaves. An old flame tries to get in touch with Audrey, and Paul is fuming to hear of Leanne's plans.
Leanne offers Paul his insurance money but, still bitter about being duped, he gives her an ultimatum. Becky and Jason arrange another date and an upbeat Kirk returns from Cyprus.
Manhunt: Drama series about a policeman in 1960s Yorkshire. Nick is taken hostage by an intruder at the Aidensfield Arms and learns of a local trade in stolen farm machinery.
Bitter Harvest: Drama series about a policeman in Yorkshire in the 1960s. Nick is called on to investigate a suspected outbreak of foot-and-mouth disease at a remote farm.
Uneasy Lies the Head: Comedy-drama series. Martin struggles to deal with Louisa's news while trying to treat a headmaster with a strange affliction that affects one of his arms.
Perish Together as Fools: Comedy-drama series. Martin suspects that PC Penhale's visiting brother may be ill, and Edith elicits an embarrassing piece of information from Louisa.
The Electric Vendetta: Feature-length drama. A ufologist claims extra-terrestrial involvement when a naked and shaved man is found in a crop circle with puncture wounds on his back.
